decisions.description: Watcher verification failed in phase 1: cmd/output_mode_boundary_test.go shows outputWorkflow and outputError render visual text instead of JSON envelopes when AETHER_OUTPUT_MODE=visual and stdout/stderr are buffered. This blocks build/plan/swarm/update/install JSON consumers and reproduces the failing code path through shouldRenderVisualOutput in cmd/codex_visuals.go.
decisions.resolution: Work completed manually — exchange tests and surveyor subtype fix both verified

decisions.description: Which existing surface should own the first implementation slice? Options: .github | Aether_Content | assets

How tightly should this work reuse existing contracts and integrations? Options: add a thin adapter around current contracts | allow a new contract if the current one blocks the goal | reuse existing contracts where possible
What should planning optimize for on the first pass? Options: architecture groundwork first | broader feature coverage first | smallest end-to-end slice first
What verification bar do you want on the first pass? Options: focused regression tests only | happy path and failure path coverage | prototype first, tighten tests after feedback
decisions.resolution: happy path and failure path coverage

decisions.description: cmd/codex_command_contract_test.go requires .aether/docs/codex-command-surface-contract.md, but the file is absent in the repo. Even after the compile break is fixed, the command-surface contract test will fail and the parity contract remains undocumented.

Fresh 'AETHER_OUTPUT_MODE=json go test ./... -count=1' is not fully runnable in this environment. Port-binding tests panic with 'listen tcp6 [::1]:0: bind: operation not permitted' in cmd/serve_test.go, pkg/downloader/downloader_test.go, and pkg/llm/client_test.go; cmd/compatibility_cmds_test.go:464 also fails to observe the expected oracle controller process tree.

Verification needed an explicit retry because ambient AETHER_OUTPUT_MODE=visual makes JSON-parsing tests fail. Example: 'AETHER_OUTPUT_MODE=visual go test ./cmd -count=1 -run TestBuildWritesDispatchArtifactsAndUpdatesState' fails at cmd/codex_build_test.go:67 with visual output instead of the expected JSON envelope.

cmd/codex_continue.go:380-384 treats the whole tests step as passed whenever the output contains any environmental marker. A mixed failure run (real regression plus sandbox/oracle issue) would be tolerated and could let aether continue advance a broken phase.

cmd/codex_continue.go:402-405 skips any failure line that matches isEnvironmentalConstraintText(), and the matcher at 777-800 treats the generic substring 'operation not permitted' as environmental. A real regression like 'panic: open fixture: operation not permitted' would be tolerated and could let aether continue advance a broken phase.
cmd/codex_worker_activity_test.go shows aether continue replaces a completed builder's honest summary with generic closure text, so the live worker record is not fully honest through continue.
